_comment: Scheduled SDL alert rules — saved to /alerts via /api/putFile. Each rule fires when its `trigger` query returns >0 rows during the lookback window (alertTime). When fired, alert events appear in the AI SIEM Alerts/Findings UI.

alerts.name: Critical OCSF Detection Findings (any source)
alerts.description: Fires when any data source reports a Detection Finding with severity_id=5 (Critical). Catches HANA Mass Exfil, SQL Injection, Linux reverse shells, Windows audit clearing, F5 WAF blocks, Palo Alto threats.
alerts.trigger: class_uid='2004' AND severity_id='5' | group n=count() by serverHost, finding_title | filter n >= 1

alerts.name: High-Severity Detection Findings
alerts.description: All HIGH severity OCSF findings — SSH brute force, sudo not in sudoers, F5 auth failures, Windows logon failures, Entra ID risky sign-ins.
alerts.trigger: class_uid='2004' AND severity_id='4' | group n=count() by serverHost, finding_title | filter n >= 1

alerts.name: Linux SSH Brute-Force Then Successful Logon (Correlation)
alerts.description: Detects 3+ failed SSH logins followed by a successful logon from the same source IP — classic credential stuffing kill chain.
alerts.trigger: serverHost='linux-ocsf' | parse 'Failed password for $f_user$ from $f_ip$' | parse 'Accepted password for $a_user$ from $a_ip$' | group fails=count(f_user), success=count(a_user) by serverHost, f_ip | filter fails >= 3 and success >= 1

alerts.name: Multi-Source Coordinated Attack (Correlation)
alerts.description: Same source IP triggers Detection Findings across 2+ different data sources within 1h — indicates coordinated multi-stage attack (e.g., port scan + brute force + exfil).
alerts.trigger: class_uid='2004' AND src_ip != null | group sources=count() by src_ip | filter sources >= 5

alerts.name: HANA Database SQL Injection or Mass Exfiltration
alerts.description: Critical SAP HANA detection: SQL injection patterns OR rows_affected > 1000 indicating data exfiltration.
alerts.trigger: serverHost='hana-ocsf' AND class_uid='2004' AND (finding_title contains 'SQL Injection' OR finding_title contains 'Mass Data Extraction')

alerts.name: DNS Suspicious Activity (BIND or Microsoft DNS)
alerts.description: BIND or Microsoft DNS detected suspicious query — security warnings, AXFR zone transfers, base64 tunneling, or dynamic DNS.
alerts.trigger: (serverHost='bind-ocsf' OR serverHost='msdns-ocsf') AND class_uid='2004' | group n=count() by finding_title | filter n >= 1

alerts.name: Cloud Identity Risky Sign-In
alerts.description: Microsoft Entra ID flagged a sign-in failure or risky activity (auth failure, high-risk, Tor exit node, etc.).
alerts.trigger: serverHost='entra-ocsf' AND class_uid='2004' | group n=count() by finding_title | filter n >= 1

alerts.name: Web Application Attack (F5 WAF)
alerts.description: F5 BIG-IP WAF blocked SQL injection, XSS, or other web attack pattern.
alerts.trigger: serverHost='f5ltm-ocsf' AND class_uid='2004' AND (finding_title contains 'WAF' OR finding_title contains 'ASM')

alerts.name: Palo Alto Threat / C2 Detection
alerts.description: Palo Alto IPS/threat engine detected vulnerability exploit, spyware/C2, or malware.

alerts.name: Windows Security Detection Finding
alerts.description: Any Windows Security Detection Finding — failed logons (4625), new accounts (4720), privileged group changes (4732), or audit log clearing (1102).
alerts.trigger: serverHost='windows-ocsf' AND class_uid='2004' | group n=count() by finding_title | filter n >= 1

alerts.name: Windows New User Account Or Privilege Escalation
alerts.description: EventID 4720 (new user) or 4732 (added to privileged group) — persistence + privesc.
alerts.trigger: serverHost='windows-ocsf' AND class_uid='2004' AND (finding_title contains '4720' OR finding_title contains '4732')

alerts.name: Authentication Failure Burst (Cross-Source)
alerts.description: 10+ authentication failures (class_uid=3002 status=Failure) across all IAM sources within 5 min — likely brute force.
alerts.trigger: class_uid='3002' AND status_id='2' | group attempts=count() by user_name | filter attempts >= 10

alerts.name: Linux Reverse Shell or Credential Dumping Tool
alerts.description: Linux process activity detected mimikatz / hashdump / kerbrute / python socket reverse shell.
alerts.trigger: serverHost='linux-ocsf' AND class_uid='2004' AND (finding_title contains 'Reverse Shell' OR finding_title contains 'Credential Dumping')

alerts.name: Network Firewall Deny / Block (Spike)
alerts.description: FortiGate or Check Point firewall denied 10+ connections — likely port scan, lateral movement attempt, or C2 callback.
alerts.trigger: (serverHost='fortigate-ocsf' OR serverHost='checkpoint-ocsf') AND class_uid='2004' | group n=count() by serverHost, src_ip | filter n >= 10

alerts.name: Darktrace AI Analyst Incident
alerts.description: Darktrace AI Analyst raised an incident (lateral movement, data exfil, suspicious SaaS activity, etc.) — top-priority NDR alert.
alerts.trigger: serverHost='darktrace-ocsf' AND class_uid='2004' AND finding_title contains 'AI Analyst' | group n=count() by finding_title | filter n >= 1

alerts.name: Darktrace Antigena Autonomous Response Triggered
alerts.description: Darktrace Antigena autonomously blocked traffic — confirms a high-confidence threat that the system already mitigated.
alerts.trigger: serverHost='darktrace-ocsf' AND class_uid='2004' AND finding_title contains 'Antigena' | group n=count() by finding_title | filter n >= 1

alerts.name: Darktrace Model Breach High Score (>=80)
alerts.description: Darktrace Model Breach with anomaly score >=80 (out of 100) — high-confidence behavioural anomaly worth investigating.
alerts.trigger: serverHost='darktrace-ocsf' AND class_uid='2004' AND finding_title contains 'Model Breach' | group n=count() by finding_title | filter n >= 1
